title: Dashboard Animation
note: The living office animations aren't rendering despite being deployed. I need this area to be alive — imagine a fish tank. The station is the tank, the agents are the fish, tasks are the food. Fish don't sit still in stagnant water. They swim, drift, feed, interact. The tank is always alive even when nothing's happening. If the current approach works, fix it. If it doesn't, rebuild it. ═══════════════════════════════ PHASE 1: DIAGNOSTIC ═══════════════════════════════ Answer with evidence, not assumptions: 1. How are sprites rendered? Canvas? DOM tags? SVG? React components? Paste the relevant 20 lines from StationRoom.tsx. 2. How are animations applied? CSS keyframes? Framer Motion? requestAnimationFrame? Canvas draw loop? 3. Open browser DevTools (F12) → Elements → find a sprite element. - Does it have animation/transition/transform CSS properties? - Console tab: any JavaScript errors? - Network tab: are the CSS files loading? Search for "sprite-working" or "ambient-pulse" in loaded stylesheets. 4. What data is reaching StationRoom? Console.log agent name, status, currentAction. Are values changing or stuck? 5. Are the CSS classes (sprite-working, sprite-sleeping, ambient-pulse, ambient-flicker) actually on the DOM elements? Do the CSS rules exist? Report findings before making changes. ═══════════════════════════════ PHASE 2: IF CSS ANIMATIONS WORK — FIX THE BUG ═══════════════════════════════ If the diagnostic shows animations are present but not firing: - Fix whatever's blocking them (missing class application, data not updating, SSR vs client mismatch) - Force a visible test: Space Monkey with green "TEST" bubble, Systems Engineer walking across the floorplan - Verify in browser DevTools that animation properties are active ═══════════════════════════════ PHASE 3: IF CSS ANIMATIONS DON'T WORK — CANVAS FISH TANK ═══════════════════════════════ If CSS animations are fundamentally unreliable, rebuild using a single HTML5 Canvas with a requestAnimationFrame loop — like an actual fish tank simulation. ARCHITECTURE: - One full-size Canvas element fills the Spacestation area - Room backgrounds drawn once as a static layer - Agent sprites drawn on top as an animated layer - Single requestAnimationFrame loop at 60fps: 1. Clear sprite layer 2. Calculate each agent's position based on state 3. Draw sprites at current positions 4. Repeat AGENT BEHAVIOUR (like fish in a tank): - IDLE: gentle drift within home room (slow random movement in a small radius, like a fish hovering). Changes direction every 5-8 seconds. Never completely still. - WORKING: moves to task location within room. Small rapid movements like a fish feeding. Status bubble appears. - WALKING: linear interpolation from current position to target room. Walking sprite frames cycle. ~2 seconds to cross between rooms. - SLEEPING: sinks slightly lower in the room. Minimal drift. Small "zzz" particles float upward and fade. - COLLABORATING: two agents move close together, face each other. Small dashes or particles flow between them. ALWAYS-ON MOVEMENT: - Even when all agents are idle, they DRIFT. Not static. Not frozen. - Like a real fish tank — no one stares at it and wonders "is it on?" - Subtle but constant. The eye catches the movement. - Room ambience (radar, sparks, lanterns, monitors) continues as background animation on the static layer. PERFORMANCE: - GPU-composited canvas (transform: translateZ(0)) - requestAnimationFrame pauses when tab hidden (Page Visibility API) - Agent positions updated each frame, sprites drawn via ctx.drawImage() with sprite sheet selection based on state - Target: 60fps, zero layout reflow DEBUG VISIBILITY: - Add a small frame counter in the corner of the canvas - Add agent position log (x, y, state) that updates visibly - This proves the animation loop is running ═══════════════════════════════ DELIVERABLE ═══════════════════════════════ After Phase 1 diagnostic: - Report what rendering method is used - Report what animation method is used - Report whether animations are present in the DOM but not visible, or completely absent Then proceed to Phase 2 or Phase 3 based on findings. If Phase 3 (canvas rebuild): - The fish tank should be visibly alive within one session - I should see agents drifting even when idle - I should see the frame counter ticking to prove the loop runs - Commit and push when working

title: Settings patch adjust
note:
Settings page fixes confirmed. The version, status, crons, and port 
labels are all correct now. 

One final check: the Run Backup button calls openclaw-backup-quick.sh. 
We know this script targets /Volumes/Public/ which may not be mounted 
(we hit the SMB permission wall). Confirm: does the button show a 
friendly error if the backup target isn't available, or does it fail 
silently?

If it fails silently, add a check to the backup script that alerts 
if the target isn't mounted — write to mount-alert.txt so Telegram 
picks it up.

After that, the Settings page audit is complete. Station is stable.

Error Feed — behaviour review and fix.
note:
Error Feed — behaviour review and fix.

The error feed currently has Investigate / Resolved / Skip buttons but 
the workflow is confusing. Here's how it should actually work:

═══════════════════════════════
CORRECT ERROR FEED WORKFLOW
═══════════════════════════════

1. A cron job fails → error appears in the Error Feed tab with a 
   "NEW" badge

2. I click "Investigate" → 
   - The error status changes to "IN PROGRESS" 
   - A corresponding task is automatically created in the Tasks 
     section under "In Progress" so the crew knows to work on it
   - The error stays visible in the feed with an in-progress indicator
   - The button changes to show "Resolved" and "Skip"

3. The crew fixes the underlying issue. The cron job runs successfully 
   on its next scheduled run →
   - The error automatically clears from the Error Feed
   - The linked task in the Tasks section moves to "Done"
   - No manual "Resolved" click needed — the system verified the fix

4. If I click "Resolved" manually →
   - For when I've verified the fix myself
   - Error disappears from the feed
   - Linked task moves to Done

5. If I click "Skip" →
   - For known non-critical issues I'm choosing to ignore
   - Error disappears from the feed
   - No task is created

6. If the same job fails again later →
   - A NEW error appears (not the old one reopened)
   - Fresh timestamp, fresh status
   - Does NOT resurrect the old skipped/resolved error

═══════════════════════════════
KEY RULES
═══════════════════════════════

- Errors should NOT show "Completed" with an "In Progress" flag 
  at the same time (current bug)
- When a cron job succeeds after being in error state, the error 
  should auto-clear — the system verified the fix
- The "Investigate" button creates a task in the Tasks board so 
  work is tracked
- The error feed reflects REALITY: if the job is still failing, 
  the error stays. If it succeeds, the error goes away.
- The cron job entry in the Scheduler list should show an error 
  badge ONLY while there's an active unresolved error

═══════════════════════════════
WHAT TO FIX NOW
═══════════════════════════════

1. Fix the double-status bug (no more "Completed" + "In Progress" 
   showing simultaneously)
2. Wire Investigate button to auto-create a task in the Tasks board
3. Wire cron job success to auto-clear the corresponding error
4. Wire cron job error badge to auto-clear when error is resolved
5. Confirm Skip behaviour works as described above
6. Test the full flow end-to-end

Report what the current behaviour actually is, then fix it to 
match this spec.  [auto-dispatched 2026-06-01 11:53]

title: Task Completion — summary and visibility improvement.
note:
Task Completion — summary and visibility improvement.

The auto-move from In Progress to Done works well. But when a task 
moves to Done, I can't tell WHAT was actually done. The card just 
says "Done" with no context.

Fix:

1. When a task completes and auto-moves to Done, the card must 
   include a brief summary of what was accomplished. This summary 
   should be:
   - Written by the agent that completed the task
   - 1-3 sentences describing the actual outcome
   - Stored on the task card itself, visible in the Kanban view
   - Also visible when I click "More Details" on the card

2. The "More Details" view on any Done card should show:
   - Original task description
   - Completion summary (what was actually done)
   - Which agent completed it
   - Timestamp of completion
   - Any relevant links or artifacts produced (files changed, 
     commits made, etc.)

3. In the Kanban view, a Done card should show at minimum:
   - Task title
   - One-line completion summary (truncated if needed)
   - "Done by [Agent] • [time]"

4. If the task was created manually by me (not auto-generated from 
   an error or cron job), the agent who picks it up should write 
   the completion summary before moving it to Done. If no summary 
   is written, the task should NOT auto-move — it should stay In 
   Progress with a flag: "Awaiting completion summary."

This applies to ALL tasks going forward. Retroactively fixing old 
Done tasks is optional — only if quick.

Report your approach before implementing.
